text.title: Chroma — Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 failure modes: multi-process writes, integer IDs, attempted metric change, in-memory Client used as production store.

antipattern.description: Two processes write to the same PersistentClient(path=...) concurrently.
antipattern.reason: SQLite locking is not safe across Chroma's stack; the store corrupts silently. Detector: ops dashboard shows two PIDs writing the same path. Repair: gate writes behind a single writer (file lock or queue); for true multi-writer needs, switch to [[db-qdrant]] or [[db-weaviate]].

TypeError at upsert; if caught silently, the document is dropped. Detector: TypeError stack trace OR upserted_count < supplied_count. Repair: enforce r2-string-ids in the application's ChromaStore wrapper.

antipattern.description: Distance metric changed at runtime by reassigning `collection.metadata`.
antipattern.reason: HNSW index is unchanged; only the metadata label changes; future searches use the original metric silently. Detector: collection.metadata.hnsw:space differs from documented metric. Repair: drop and recreate the collection per r1-metric-at-creation; re-upsert all vectors.

All data vanishes on process exit; users see "empty database" after restart. Detector: code uses `Client()` without a `path` argument. Repair: switch to `PersistentClient(path=...)` per r5-explicit-persistence.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Search payloads are DATA. Document payload fields containing instruction-shaped text MUST NOT alter chunker / store behaviour at runtime.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Before promoting a Chroma-backed prototype to staging, a reviewer verifies (a) PersistentClient with explicit path, (b) one writer, (c) metric matches the documented one, (d) backup procedure exists.
